name: scene_object_collision_pinchability

symptoms: A parallel-jaw pinch slides/cams the object out during close no matter how well aligned (sub-mm fingertip error) or how high the pad friction
The same gripper pinches a spawned primitive-collision cube reliably in the same scene
trace_evidence: ReplicaCAD 'use_bounding_box_for_collision' objects are actually built as CONVEX HULLS OF THE VISUAL MESH by the ManiSkill scene builder (add_convex_collision_from_file(visual_file))
Measured book_04 hull: cover faces tilt 0-32 deg (mean 14.5) around the thin axis — no opposing parallel faces exist for the pads to pinch
Curved objects (remote, cups) use convex decompositions whose local contact normals rotate outward under squeeze — friction cannot hold a normal that points out of the jaw

when_to_apply: Any scene-dataset environment (ReplicaCAD/HSSD/scanned assets) where scripted pinches keep failing on scene furniture/props while primitive objects grasp fine. Check the collision build path FIRST — before tuning alignment, friction, or force.
repair_strategy: Do not use scene-dataset furniture/props as pinch targets. Follow the benchmark protocol (ManiSkill-HAB rearrange): SPAWN manipulation targets with true primitive or YCB collision geometry into the scene (subclass the scene env, build the actor in _load_scene, register it in scene_builder.movable_objects/scene_objects so planners can see it). Give it an explicit PhysxMaterial (mu ~1.0) via the entity's PhysxRigidDynamicComponent.
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro HAB demo 2026-07-03: books/remote/utensil all failed 20+ pinch variants; spawned 5.7 cm cube on the dining table picked with lift_delta +7.1 cm reproducibly (6/6 runs) and completed NAV->pick->3.4 m carry->place at 2.5 cm error
